page: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v4 is an EASv4-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. The configuration includes 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The processor is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

This server is highly memory-optimized, providing 256.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 16.0 GB of memory per core. For local storage, it includes a 524 GB temporary disk and is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disks. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

Performance benchmarks place the E32-16as_v4 in the strong tier for single-core CPU performance via stress-ng, scoring 2707.19. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the average tier. Large block uncached memory read bandwidth is recorded at 51.28 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is 30.24 GB/sec. Database operations score 6814.49 on PassMark, and Redis SET operations reach 16.2 million operations per second. Small-scale LLM inference using a 135M model yields 180.3 tokens per second for text generation, while a medium 7B model processes text generation at 12.25 tokens per second.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16:1, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The combination of 256 GB of RAM, dedicated AMD EPYC cores, and Premium Storage capability makes this instance well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v4 is an EASv4-family virtual machine built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores running at 3.525 GHz. The instance is heavily memory-optimized, offering 256.0 GB of RAM alongside 524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. While multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall within the average tier, its single-core CPU performance is strong, scoring 2707.19 in stress-ng tests. With a high 16:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads. It is highly suitable for hosting relational databases, in-memory caches such as Redis, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v4 is an AMD EPYC-powered virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

tagline: An AMD EPYC virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio.
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
